details.shortText: Brijesh Sharma to Sai Sudharsan, OUT
details.text: Oh my, B Sai Sudharsan is hit wicket twice in two innings! This is unbeleivable! Low full toss outside off and he drives it with an open face. The ball goes away fine on the off side for four, but in his follow through, the bat goes flying out of his hands and lands on the stumps. Gill is on his haunches, he can't believe it, and neither can Sai Sudharsan! Even Jurel is sympathetic as he comes over and hands SaiSu his bat back, and gives him a pat on the shoulder

Oh, that looks plumb, and it's given! Gill misses his flick and Archer raps him on the pads. This was full around off and swerving in slightly. Muted celebrations from Archer and RR as Gill walks back after having all but won the game for his team. It's a cliche, but that was a "captain's knock"

Washington miscues it and Ferreira grabs a good catch. Tries to take on this length ball outside off, but it's a cutter and he's early into the shot. Goes high in the air and Ferreira at long-on has to run in and to his left to get under it, and the South African makes no mistake

Placement fails Jaiswal, he launched a pull, seemed like he wanted to get it much finer but top-edged it to deep-backward square. 139ks short of length at the hips, shot was on but it'll be a first-over wicket for Siraj

wicket.shortText: c Prasidh Krishna b Mohammed Siraj

details.text: Pitched up and Rabada strikes! He went full and on middle and off, 146ks and may have seamed away a touch. Jurel stood on leg and went for a lofted off drive. Bat-face opened and it floated high to mid-off.

Slot ball, caught at the boundary. Parag, expressionless, stands still as Holder upper-cuts the air in celebration. This was full on leg at 132ks, flicked it a touch too early, toed it to deep midwicket.

The short-ball ploy has worked so well for Gujarat Titans today! Shanaka has feathered it behind! Banged in short outside off and angling into Shanaka, who looks for the pull. He's cramped for room and gloves it behind and it's bread, butter, eggs and bacon for Jos Buttler!

Edged, and taken. The Archer experiment is done as GT celebrate a fifth wicket! Test match stuff, a length ball outside off that straightens off the pitch, drawing Archer into the drive. Archer goes at it at full tilt and gets a thick edge and Buttler does the rest

Oh boy, Sooryavanshi falls short by four runs this time! He was out for 97 in the last game, he goes for 96 today. Silence all around as Sooryavanshi looks to tennis-smash this short ball. Like in the last game, this ends up going up off the toe end and it's a simple catch at deep third, where Prasidh takes the catch. Very similar to how he got out in the last game, and he's distraught. The silence soon makes way for applause as he walks off

Oh my, B Sai Sudharsan is hit wicket twice in two innings! This is unbeleivable! Low full toss outside off and he drives it with an open face. The ball goes away fine on the off side for four, but in his follow through, the bat goes flying out of his hands and lands on the stumps. Gill is on his haunches, he can't believe it, and neither can Sai Sudharsan! Even Jurel is sympathetic as he comes over and hands SaiSu his bat back, and gives him a pat on the shoulder

Like in the last game, this ends up going up off the toe end and it's a simple catch at deep third, where Prasidh takes the catch. Very similar to how he got out in the last game, and he's distraught. The silence soon makes way for applause as he walks off

Edged, and taken. The Archer experiment is done as GT celebrate a fifth wicket! Test match stuff, a length ball outside off that straightens off the pitch, drawing Archer into the drive. Archer goes at it at full tilt and gets a thick edge and Buttler does the rest

Edged, and taken. The Archer experiment is done as GT celebrate a fifth wicket! Test match stuff, a length ball outside off that straightens off the pitch, drawing Archer into the drive. Archer goes at it at full tilt and gets a thick edge and Buttler does the rest

Straight to mid-on but Venky Iyer has done his job! Siraj cramps the batter for room who was once again backing away. He goes deep in the crease but Siraj rushes him for pace with the good length ball at 147.5kph on leg. He mistimes to Rabada who makes no mistake

CARVED to deep third! That is wicket No. 29 for Rabada which wins him the purple cap. He gives himself room but Rabada bowls this fullish and outside off. Padikkal has to reach for it, and he ends up slicing it to the left of Arshad, who runs to his left and takes a smart catch

Rabada takes soft steps back... and takes the catch on the long-on boundary! He raised his right leg upon taking it but the back leg was still after he took it above head. Spin-hitter Patidar saw a full ball, got on one knee and swept it from outside off. Got a lot of height on it, nearly got it over. Rashid strikes second ball

And Rashid has brought it back to life! Krunal has a chat with Kohli but doesn't review! Loopy full ball on off, Krunal moves across to off, clears the front leg out of the way and goes for a hard sweep. The legbreak spins in a touch to beat bat and hit the back leg in front of off. Krunal swipes his bat in frustration on the way back, Rashid and co celebrate. Hawk-Eye shows that'd have smashed middle and off

That looks out. Arshad convinced and then in disbelief. Buttler threw the ball up. Quick sign to review from GT. 144ks length ball landed on leg and finished on middle and off. David was in the crease, front leg slightly away as he looked to defend straight. The ball took a feather while angling away, Ultra-Edge confirms. Ball still seaming, Sid Monga says. David gets a pat on the shoulder from Kohli

Straight up, straight up and Gill is out of here. The aggression of Gill goes against him this time around. Back of a length ball outside off, Gill stays inside his crease this time to the 142kph delivery. He looks to go across the line, gets it on the higher part of the bat, with the ball spooning up. Patidar runs to his right from mid-off and takes a good catch

The bouncer has brought about Sai Sudharsan's downfall. Banged in short outside off stump, a very well-directed short ball from Bhuvi. Sai Sudharsan has to fetch that 138.6kph delivery from outside off, he gets a top-edge with the ball ballooning to the short fine leg region. Jitesh runs behind it and takes a comfortable catch

Straight down Padikkal's throat at long-on. Another one bites the dust as Sindhu walks back. Rising back of a length ball on middle at 132kph. He charges down the track but is not in control as he goes for the pull. Also loses his balance in playing that shot with the ball only going as far as DDP who is stationed perfectly.

Five-head Krunal outfoxes Buttler! Sees him dance down, so he yorks him wide. 114ks, lands in line with Buttler's back-knee after beating bat. Buttler's upper body froze late as he reached out, trying to scythe the ball away. Too late and Krunal celebrates with intensity. GT's big three toppled!

Forget Ben, It's Josh cutting off Arshad's attack. Trademark back-of-length ball skidded and rushed Arshad. He was back in the crease, swivelling for the pull. Ball came under belly-height. He got cramped - upper-body bent down, elbows weren't extended and the top edge went straight to short fine leg.

Tewatia goes and the crowd go up bigtime! 131ks length ball angling in towards fourth stump from around the wicket. The cross-seamer seemed to lose pace off the pitch and bounced a touch low. Tewatia extended his arms and looked to lift it over mid-on with a mostly horizontal bat. Ball hit low on the bat and Patidar ran back to take a rather easy catch. Rasikh is pumped, he's had a great game so far

Top edge. Will there be confusion? No, Hazlewood keeps the keeper at bay and allows Bhuvi to get to 28 wickets this season! 128ks short ball around fifth stump, seam up. Seemed slow off the pitch but bounced good. Holder was square-on, jumped and looked to drag it across with a pull. Edged it right back, Jitesh ran straight but Hazlewood ran across from short third and took it. Seemed to have made the call early as Jitesh slowed down

details.shortText: Rasikh Salam to Rashid Khan, OUT
details.text: Rashid goes again... can't clear the boundary this time! Went across the ground by pulling the scrambled-seam ball. 132ks. Bat went from slightly down to up, just over the shoulder. Got it a touch too high. Shepherd takes it to the right at deep-backward square
